name: The White House
subject: ‘We need to build FAST’
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=42b2ad2ebf6eea46b19bbae7c6d9e7f59f5d79f7816cb63c7816e7dbd8fef918037ae2005d100432b39fe04280da250c6bfaa49d3bd257da

‘We need to build FAST’

Five of President Donald J. Trump’s Cabinet secretaries traveled to Capitol Hill yesterday to testify before the Senate Commerce Committee on the pressing need to rebuild American infrastructure. Transportation Secretary Elaine Chao, Commerce Secretary Wilbur Ross, Labor Secretary Alex Acosta, Agriculture Secretary Sonny Perdue, and Energy Secretary Rick Perry represented the Administration.

“We need to build FAST & we need to build for our FUTURE,” President Trump tweeted. He also thanked the Commerce Committee for hosting this important hearing.

One month ago, the President laid out his six principles for building a stronger America:

1. Using $200 billion in Federal funds to spur at least $1.5 trillion in infrastructure investments from States, localities, and the private sector

2. Making new investments in rural America, which has been left behind for too long

3. Returning decision-making authority to State and local governments

4. Removing regulatory barriers that needlessly get in the way of projects

5. Streamlining and shortening the permitting process

6. Supporting and strengthening America’s workforce

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b2d66f10bf105edca2b5f692a710fb08065a57a76fe0f585a58889fb641f8375d4b132a83a5aabcc880f81decf957d5f788c76651a773ae3
Learn more about how President Trump wants to rebuild America.
Bonus:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b2d66f10bf105edcb2f178d05a8c34a28fc106515effef2d018ae305baeaa45858aae09b7a4dbe81e2b13597a2c524ed65e31ee5ae9f3769
See the 1-minute highlight reel from yesterday’s hearing on Capitol Hill.

Tax cuts come home

Yesterday, President Trump joined business leaders and workers in Missouri to discuss what tax cuts have done for the economy since they were signed into law just before Christmas.

As a result of tax reform, more than 430 companies have announced pay raises, bonuses, or 401(k) hikes that benefit more than 4 million Americans. In Missouri specifically, companies such as Central Bancompany, Hawthorn Bank, and Kansas City Southern have announced bonuses of up to $1,000 for workers.

“Passing tax reform was just the latest signal from the president that America is once again open for business and that he is committed to ensuring we remain the world’s most vibrant economy,” Missouri Lt. Gov. Mike Parson wrote this week. “Jobs are being created and companies are investing more in growth because they know they can trust Trump to make the right decisions.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b2d66f10bf105edcc00fc1692447214724bc1c286856f29644683b9503503dd23a7632bc20fdfd8b1c2661a3e9bc25556e5c7c1cf0f44473
Read Lt. Gov. Parson’s op-ed in full here.

More:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b2d66f10bf105edc183044e467d2938ff08ecbcdfba474bbba581c88e5075b81e1764ff74613ff206a9cc0807564ff5b5632b28cfb7a45e5
President Trump’s tax cuts are a windfall for Americans.

St. Patrick’s Day and Irish heritage in America

Today, Ireland’s Taoiseach—Ireland’s term for its head of government—is visiting the White House ahead of St. Patrick’s Day. Leo Varadkar, who has served as Taoiseach since last June, continues a long tradition of visits from Ireland’s leader to the United States to mark the international Irish holiday.

The Taoiseach’s visit offers a moment to reflect on the importance of the Irish people and their descendants to American history. These contributions date back to our Nation’s beginning: At least eight signers of the Declaration of Independence, including four of the Founding Fathers, had Irish roots.

“As we spend this month honoring Irish Americans, we also pledge to further strengthen our relationship with the Emerald Isle itself,” President Trump wrote in his Proclamation honoring Irish-American Heritage Month.

Go deeper:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b2d66f10bf105edc6838a47389419452ce26cddf8452be4c6c28d6995c6e839e79465e37750675974431af39cafadd01fa313fc50ef6cb29
St. Patrick’s Day and Irish heritage in American history

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump at the Boeing Building 75 | March 14, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump welcomes Prime Minister Leo Varadkar of Ireland to the White House. The two leaders, along with Vice President Mike Pence, will participate in an expanded bilateral meeting. The President and Vice President will then travel to the U.S. Capitol to attend the Friends of Ireland luncheon.

In the afternoon, the President and Vice President will meet with members of the American Petroleum Institute. Shortly after, the President will meet with Bill Gates.

This evening, the President, Vice President, and First Lady will welcome Prime Minister Varadkar back to the White House to participate in the Shamrock Bowl Presentation.

body:
Peter -- We’re touching base on
where we are re: next week’s primary. Our final fundraising
deadline is tomorrow night and we’re still working towards our goal.

All the contributions we receive are going right back out the
door to support our volunteers and GOTV efforts.

Can you chip in now to help us meet our goal? It’s never been
more important than right now.
( https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dl-marmm?refcode=DL_EM_FR_WL_180315_1_2&recurring=auto&amount=10.00&firstname=%7B%7BFirstName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&lastname=%7B%7BLastName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&addr1=%7B%7BAddressLine1,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&addr2=%7B%7BAddressLine2,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&email=%7B%7BRecipientEmailAddress,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&zip=%7B%7BZipCode,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&city=%7B%7BCity,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&state=%7B%7BState,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&phone=%7B%7BPhoneNumber,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&occupation=%7B%7BOccupation,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&employer=%7B%7BEmployer,DefaultTo=%7D%7D )

Begin forwarded message:

Peter — We’re less than a week away
from our March 20 primary — this is it.

Our team is working around the clock to reach voters,
mobilize volunteers, and make the preparations for the biggest
GOTV effort we can possibly mount.

I’m reaching out because this Friday night is our final
fundraising deadline before the primary. We need at least $17,000
to fully fund our GOTV efforts.

We need you now more than ever before. This is do or die —
can you chip in $5, $10, or $20 to help us close the gap and
support our field staff? Click here to make your contribution.
( https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dl-marmm?refcode=DL_EM_FR_WL_180315_1_2&recurring=auto&amount=10.00&firstname=%7B%7BFirstName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&lastname=%7B%7BLastName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&addr1=%7B%7BAddressLine1,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&addr2=%7B%7BAddressLine2,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&email=%7B%7BRecipientEmailAddress,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&zip=%7B%7BZipCode,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&city=%7B%7BCity,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&state=%7B%7BState,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&phone=%7B%7BPhoneNumber,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&occupation=%7B%7BOccupation,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&employer=%7B%7BEmployer,DefaultTo=%7D%7D )

What we do right now will determine the future of our
district.

I’ve spent my entire career fighting for the working
families of the Southwest Side. I don’t plan to stop now. The
people I support know they can rely on me. That’s why the
Illinois AFL-CIO, the Tribune, the Sun-Times, and 30 mayors from
towns and villages in our district have all given our campaign
their full endorsement.

Right now, we need your help to close this fundraising gap
and support our staff. In these FINAL DAYS before the primary,
can we count on you to support us? Please contribute here before
midnight Friday to help us out.
( https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dl-marmm?refcode=DL_EM_FR_WL_180315_1_2&recurring=auto&amount=10.00&firstname=%7B%7BFirstName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&lastname=%7B%7BLastName,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&addr1=%7B%7BAddressLine1,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&addr2=%7B%7BAddressLine2,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&email=%7B%7BRecipientEmailAddress,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&zip=%7B%7BZipCode,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&city=%7B%7BCity,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&state=%7B%7BState,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&phone=%7B%7BPhoneNumber,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&occupation=%7B%7BOccupation,DefaultTo=%7D%7D&employer=%7B%7BEmployer,DefaultTo=%7D%7D )

I’m very thankful to have you all by my side.

Thanks,

Dan

Thanks for your support of our campaign.

deductible.

There's no denying it, friends: So far, the president's policies are a complete and utter catastrophe for middle-class Americans.

Whether he's sabotaging our health care system that so many rely on or trying to gut the social safety net in order to fund a tax scheme that mostly benefits the wealthy, President Trump's agenda is one hell of a disaster for everyday folks.

Our president is supposed to work _for_ the American people - not against us. **So** **I'm fighting every day in the Senate to hold President Trump accountable, but right now, I need to know where you stand:**

Well, we have a special place in our heart for Virginia as well - and we believe it is ready to reject what Donald Trump stands for yet again. Let's make sure our strong grassroots coalition sends a message in 2018 that Trump can't ignore.

name: Amanda Brown Lierman, Democrats.org
subject: Here's what made Conor's victory possible:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
Conor Lamb's win in Pennsylvania is proof that we need to be organizing and competing everywhere.
---
Derek --

Tuesday night, Conor Lamb won in a district that Republicans controlled for nearly two decades -- one that Trump won by nearly 20 points!

When we say we're fighting in the reddest districts and states to help candidates like Conor win elections, we mean it. Democrats can win anywhere as long as we organize -- and our work is just getting started.

After Tuesday night, there's not a single area of the country that I'm afraid to compete in. But it all comes down to this: If Democrats are going to continue flipping seats from red to blue, we need your help. So I'm asking you today:

**Will you chip in $3 right now to help elect more Democrats up and down the ballot this year?**

** Part of the reason why Conor's win is so impressive is that he raised money from lots of people chipping in what they could afford. More than 14,000 of you contributed directly to Conor's campaign, helping him build a strong, on-the-ground organizing program.

This victory makes one thing clear: There is no substitute for grassroots organizing.

This race was supposed to be an easy win for the GOP. But thanks to folks like you, Democrats picked up a critical seat in the House.

name: Michael Lighty, The Sanders Institute
subject: "Healthcare Not Insurance"
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
We need healthcare, not insurance.
The fight for healthcare is not new. Americans have been fighting industry and crony capitalism for quality healthcare for decades. Our first achievements were historical--we stopped child labor in conditions that would haunt their health for the rest of their lives, we ensured a weekend to allow American families to rest and recuperate, we ensured that many companies offered healthcare benefits to entice the best workers. Unions led those fights for American rights. In areas with strong unions, health outcomes were better, pay was better for all, and more Americans were satisfied with their jobs.
With the recent decline of unions, so too has our ability to fight for our healthcare in the same way. Decades of incremental erosion of healthcare benefits, escalating costs, political efforts to undermine unions, and the thousands of strikes over just keeping the health plans workers have fought to win has taken a huge toll on the quality of those plans and on the unions themselves.
Crony capitalism is winning, and our access to quality, affordable healthcare is receding.
In my article [**A Labor-Based Movement For Medicare for All**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7acaa/bed43eb/5f7dad87/75fe3d51/1111035776/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/a-labor-based-movement-for-medicare-for-all"), I argue that we are losing this battle but we do not have to lose the war. However, to do so, we must change how we fight.
Every American wants for them and their families to be healthy, but why do we automatically assume that insurance is a part of that equation? We should not be fighting for access to insurance, we should be fighting for our access to healthcare. We should not put our health and our lives in the hands of insurance companies.
Medicare has worked well in this country to provide healthcare to our aging citizens. We have the opportunity and the ability to expand this program, get rid of the corporate middle-man, and to create a system that allows all Americans access to quality healthcare. A Medicare for All system is our new tactic in this battle for American health and American justice.
We can change the game. As we have seen with the Fight for $15 and the #MeToo movement, we have the power to overcome politically powerful economic forces by mobilizing public opinion through our work.
In demanding guaranteed healthcare through a Medicare for All system, we are demanding a more just and healthy, productive and humane society. Medicare for All not only motivates millions to organize for justice, but winning it would help win justice for all.
[**Read my article**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7acaa/bed43eb/5f7dad87/75fe3d51/1111035776/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/a-labor-based-movement-for-medicare-for-all") and share with your friends, and start a conversation.
Thanks for staying engaged,
Michael Lighty
Director of Public Policy for National Nurses United & Fellow of The Sanders Institute

What are the [issues](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=85b9818f5e14435a9b69f9cc8f9d3132&_e=P-ULxzxaRLY-kNk4Tqlvgg10WZ-evdwwzjMIq1t-pO1HoqVSmCyZ1bVVlVjg0-k3-JbIy6ssAQmLMxY06TTTsOf4LYNsjTDs3ZjAdlonScPgnUSBA7p7_ztr44BAbbVRG9Tq5E5T0-VpigxMb8LZ4zH9ZTzUZVJn9hY6yV7jqVSUQIsxLujD_mTS39elC0vrcG1TJJLXt6apKTn1KVGCRMSSuRBYX4dIRjF9DxQu9BfKl2X9CC9wu6UULF9qwTqWv9UVNFNmOskhFzWq8HkIL5gU2ex61EQ6ZNQ9HIHi-RIYepABzbM4Koe0rVnKOTMMM5TIRF6O2q3xxdNpPZmpt88ddzo23bZDo3vapRPg3vwWSxgcdgEtTY9chXTVH-UM) that you care about? If the past few years have taught me anything, it’s that if you want to make change, you have to stand up and lead. So I’m asking you, to [stand up](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=85b9818f5e14435a9b69f9cc8f9d3132&_e=P-ULxzxaRLY-kNk4Tqlvgg10WZ-evdwwzjMIq1t-pO1psfE86dWyTOOA7RfFQBs-l2DKAPvnOEUZom2WXrzWjy5UlQUDnZpssKZ-NTFtJbr32Im-S5Qv1yEChsekkA3zxDJL2rmLiE8ffP4diEqHoqsr3pC6COwIOaM_IbmfrQDn0sCdCfWEMqEM-Dkvaut7CqeaKy9v7JPI8mFJ_U5zthCOzDl-YvjhY3l6Nbc_-rnAE6iX0rKTmSATgvfLK2E48V8xD41rrtGzToAPiTaUvfVrKPq60s_vOtAdQrOlCVyiXRDfs3_gKIounQ4_1yKL0CDI8DyykgX2Nt1Xa0-u-RZ3TwoOyAxN8386B_q4En6BYFeGiE6YW_tuVzQ6n-BL) to help me take the Wisconsin way to Washington. clean_body: I've enjoyed meeting new faces and helping share our hope to finally bring an end to Washington dysfunction. Every stop on our tour has been a...... Friend, We've been out touring Wisconsin to win the support of grassroots voters and friend, it's been great! I've enjoyed meeting new faces and helping share our hope to finally bring an end to Washington dysfunction. Every stop on our tour has been a reminder that D.C. could use more of our Wisconsin way! Just this week, we crossed 50,000 miles. There's just one little hitch... The gas bill is starting to add up. So, I’m reaching out for help. As we get further and further along, it’s more important than ever that we build our support throughout Wisconsin. That means this tour MUST go on. If everyone getting this email can chip in just $5, we'll be able to fill up the whole tank and share our message with hundreds of more Wisconsinites! I really appreciate your support, friend. You've heard it before but I’ll say it again: it's grassroots support that’ll add up to make all the difference in our race. Thanks, Leah Paid for by Leah for Senate

subject: STL Police Chief Demands Answers About 'Odd' Investigation From Circuit Attorney Kim Gardner
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
Team,

The St. Louis Post-Dispatch reported yesterday new details about the lead investigator working for Kim Gardner: he was under FBI investigation.

But also in the piece were the below comments from the St. Louis Police Chief calling into question Gardner's decision-making. Please share with your friends on Facebook and Twitter .
---

By Robert Patrick, Joel Currier and Christine Byers
March 14, 2018
Link to full article (http://www.stltoday.com/news/local/crime-and-courts/man-investigating-greitens-affair-was-himself-investigated-by-the-fbi/article_fd9ad552-57f7-5725-839d-5aa64432925a.html?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=e571c32881-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_03_08&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-e571c32881-99411629)

St. Louis police Chief John Hayden disputed claims by Ryan that he declined to investigate...

“It makes me frustrated because it’s as if she’s saying I failed to do something I’m supposed to do here, and that’s not the case at all,” Hayden said. “To date, nobody has tried to report this to the police department.”

“I didn’t know Kim Gardner had identified this person until she said (in media reports) she’s conducting her own investigation,” Hayden said.

In his more than 30 years with the department, Hayden said he was unaware of a prosecutor using a private firm to investigate a local crime.

“This has all been oddly done to me as far as I’m concerned,” Hayden said. “She has to explain why she did what she did.”

name: Anne Holton
subject: Long but important email (re: House vote yesterday)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
Derek -

Yesterday, the House of Representatives voted on legislation that _originally included a provision arming teachers in our school classrooms._ What once was laughed off as a classic Trumpian comment is now actually something the Republican Congress considered.

_Students aren 't really asking for this._ They're walking out of schools across the country to show their support for increasing the age limits to buy dangerous weapons, for universal background checks, and for bans on the kind of assault weapons used to carry out these mass shootings in our schools.

_Teachers aren 't really asking for this._ They already have enough on their plate - paying for supplies out of their own pockets, dealing with ever-present learning challenges, helping spark an interest in math or science - and they're paid too little for it. Now, they're going to be asked to carry a gun and be a security force?

When I was Virginia's Secretary of Education, I was in a lot of conversations about what we are teaching our children, but in this case I want to talk about what our children are teaching us. [**They are growing up in such an increasingly political time, and they are forming their own opinions and asking us to move our schools, our states, and our country forward**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=k3%2BgvjXGdhT3KbiKRofBxuOwX09Gf%2BLG) \- which is exactly what Tim and I have always believed in doing.

You could say that making Virginia more progressive has been a project of Tim's and mine for the last 33 years. Together, we have worked to build on the legacy of service my family began many years ago and have each, in our own ways, thought about what we could personally do to make this a Commonwealth for all people.

For me, the classroom has always been a place where we can make big strides for our future. It's where my father took on powerful interests to champion civil rights and school desegregation. He even sent me and my siblings to a majority-African American school to show how seriously he took the cause of progress. The classroom is where the next generation spends its days - and when change is needed, their voices are often the first ones we should listen to.

So while it is disheartening to watch Congress ignore the will of students and teachers - particularly at this moment, when so many are standing up and demanding change - I wanted to send you a note to make sure you know that Tim will continue to fight to move us forward. We will fight for gun violence prevention, for Virginia's teachers, for all the progressive values that will help us continue to move this Commonwealth forward. It's the family business, after all.

Thanks for reading,

_Anne_

P.S. This month Tim and his team are counting number of donations, _not amounts._ We appreciate any and every way you wish to support our campaign and our family's vision for Virginia, whether it's donating or posting on social media or talking to your friends and neighbors. But I'd be remiss if I didn't ask you to chip in this month before the big filing deadline. [If you wish to chip in today, enter any amount on this page >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=ODcWLm4QBsblpKCX2Od2jeOwX09Gf%2BLG)
---

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: A Christian student is silenced in America
body:
| | Christians can stop this...but will we?
---
[ ](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/p180314apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180315apl01ndb&n=1)
| Peter, Chike Uzuegbunam, a student on Georgia Gwinnett College, wanted to share the good news of Jesus with others at his school, but he was prohibited from doing so outside the campus library because it wasn’t a “free speech” zone. And the next time Chike spoke, standing in a free-speech zone with an approved free-speech permit in hand, campus police silenced his speech because they deemed the information he shared as “disorderly conduct” because it “disturbed the comfort” of listeners. In other words, this public college, receiving taxpayer funds, simply didn’t like Chike’s speech so they silenced it. It is frightening to see this taking place on a campus in America. The Department of Justice filed a “statement of interest” in a federal lawsuit against Georgia Gwinnett College’s “speech zone” policy and just recently the Georgia state legislature begun debating free-speech zone policies. The threat to religious free speech isn’t isolated to Georgia. It is a real threat in most every state and on many campuses across America. **It’s crucial we put into office legislators and those in governance who are committed to protecting religious free speech, and for this to happen Christians must vote!** Peter, we have a plan to ensure millions of Christians vote in the thousands of elections taking place this year. Through Public Service Announcements on TV and Radio, an aggressive social media campaign, and our church and university outreach, we can motivate and equip millions of Christians – but we can’t do it without you. Primaries across the nation are taking place. We must engage as many Christians as possible before the elections! **[Please prayerfully consider making your best gift to My Faith Votes today.](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/p180315apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180315apl01ndb&n=2)** I hope I can count on you to be part of defending religious freedom in America. **United. We Stand.**

Gov. Mike Huckabee
Honorary National Chairman
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180315apl01ndb&n=3) [](https://www3-myfaithvotes.nationbuilder.com/p180315apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180315apl01ndb&n=4)

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Bring it on
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | Friends, I'm up against five opponents and "substantial GOP money" in this re-election campaign. Pundits have written about it. News sources reported it. There's a lot of work to do to win. You know what I say to that? **_LET 'S GO._** I don't back down in the face of a challenge. When I first ran for office, I was up against six primary opponents. People said it couldn't be done. _The one thing I learned ...don't believe conventional wisdom and get to work._ I hit the pavement, knocking on door after door. I walked every neighborhood in the district. I walked so much my husband Steve, worried about all the weight I lost, handed me a milkshake every morning. By Election Day, I'd knocked on 25,000 doors -- and I won. Since then, we've faced many challenges. When it comes to passing the Affordable Care Act. protecting Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security or securing funds for Planned Parenthood, my voice will not be silenced. When it comes to attacking Donald Trump and his radical agenda, I will not shy away from speaking out, no matter what attacks come my way. South Florida families rely on me to fight for them. It's my job -- and I'm proud to give it all I have. **But I can 't do it alone. I rely on you to help.** With your help, we can outwork the opposition and beat the GOP money. We cannot back down from this challenge -- not even for a second. [_**We need to reach our $30,000 FEC goal by the deadline. Our mid-month deadline is tomorrow night. And I need you to chip in what you can, even if it's $5 or $10, to get us there.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113f9a/622b7b5b/19aae26e/307b8f13/1436915006/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse180315?refcode=dwse180315_b&recurring=true&amount=5") Thank you. Debbie
